channel.title: Pulkit Goyal - Thoughts
channel.description: Questions, frameworks, and notes in progress from Pulkit Goyal.

item.title: Stop Making Me Update the Tool
item.description: We've normalized spending 5-10% of our time documenting progress in systems that don't help us do the actual work. Project management should not interrupt the work.

item.title: When Learning Feels Like Learning, Something's Wrong
item.description: Schools teach before students care, but learning without need is just forgetting with extra steps.

item.title: Why Your Phone Has Multiple Cameras (And Why They're Arranged That Way)
item.description: The camera bump on your phone isn't arbitrary. The placement and arrangement of the cameras reveal which trade-offs the manufacturer decided were worth it.

item.title: You Won't Own a Car and You'll Be Happy
item.description: Self-driving cars will make owning a car financially senseless. But being happy without owning one requires giving up more than just the economics.

item.title: Are You Designing for Flexibility or Learner Interaction?
item.description: As online learning becomes more flexible, the chances for learners to feel genuinely connected to one another quietly diminish.
